title: Game Design: The Medium Is the Message
creator: Jonathan Blow
description: A public lecture about the ideas games communicate through rules, rewards, and repeated actions, and about the responsibility involved in choosing what a game asks its players to practice.
useful: It is an accessible entry into Blow's broader cultural argument, with enough concrete game examples to support agreement, criticism, or comparison with other design traditions.

manualLinkStatus.note: CreativeMornings video opened successfully during editorial review.

audience: Developers, students, and general audiences looking for a clear introduction to games as expressive systems rather than neutral entertainment products.
learning: Identify messages carried by rules and reward structures
Discuss artistic responsibility without reducing games to stories
Question what repeated player actions normalize or rehearse
